description: The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) is the official UK government body responsible for intellectual property (IP) rights including patents, designs, trade marks and copyright. IPO is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology , supported by 1 public body .

details.body:
The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) is the official UK government body responsible for intellectual property (IP) rights including patents, designs, trade marks and copyright.

IPO is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology, supported by 1 public body.

image.alt_text: Collage of IPO staff within the office

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) has published its Corporate Plan for 2026 to 2027, setting out its priorities and commitments for the year ahead.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Intellectual Property Office publishes Corporate Plan 2026 to 2027

ordered_featured_documents.summary: From chocolate bars to famous cars, and the mark where it all began – Britain crowns its best loved trade marks.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Poll reveals public’s most iconic trade marks as register turns 150

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Today the IPO has launched a suite of new digital services to apply for, manage or renew UK patents.
ordered_featured_documents.title: New digital patents services have launched

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Three-year funding commitment alongside new industry partnership pilot.
ordered_featured_documents.title: IPO confirms continued funding for specialist IP crime unit

ordered_featured_documents.summary: As we prepare to launch the One IPO patents service, we are sharing our roadmap of planned changes, improvements and enhancements over the coming months.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Introducing the One IPO Roadmap

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Customers should not pay these, and should always report them to the IPO.
ordered_featured_documents.title: IPO issues fresh warning to beware of  misleading invoices

ordered_featured_links.title: Types and uses of intellectual property
Get intellectual property rights: step by step

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information.

details.body:
The Acting Chief Executive is the leader of the IPO and is responsible for the day-to-day management of the organisation.

The Comptroller General is formally responsible for the operation of the IPO and supervising the quality of its accounting and financial reporting.

role.title: Acting Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

role_appointments.title: Adam Williams - Acting Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

details.body:
The chief executive is the leader of the IPO and is responsible for the day-to-day management of the organisation.

role.title: Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

role_appointments.title: Adam Williams - Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

details.body:
This role is responsible for the IPO’s international activities, supporting the UK’s trade ambitions, developing IP skills in business, research institutions, education and wider government, and ensuring effective interaction between the IP system and other regulatory frameworks.

This includes responsibility for the IPO’s relationships with multilateral institutions such as WIPO, and the office’s bilateral relationships and cooperation with key global markets.  The UK’s IP attaché network is a key part of this work, providing support and raising awareness for UK businesses who are exporting or are looking to export.

The Directorate also leads on IP enforcement issues. This includes establishing a national centre of excellence for IP intelligence, working in partnership with law enforcement agencies and taking a central coordination role to tackle IP crime in the UK and internationally.

role.title: Director of Enforcement, Business and International Directorate

role_appointments.title: Adam Williams - Director of Business and International Policy

details.body: The Deputy Chief Executive is responsible for the UK national patent, trade mark and registered design systems.

role.title: Deputy Chief Executive and Director of Services, IPO

role_appointments.title: Andy Bartlett - Deputy Chief Executive, IPO

details.body:
The Digital Data & Technology Directorate is central to the IPO’s journey of transformation and is charged with the creation of a world leading technology platform and service that can provide businesses and citizens with the tools they need to harness the power of IP.

The CDDTO ensures that the IPO’s strategic ambitions are fully supported by its tech capability and operating model.

This includes leading a committed, multi-disciplinary digital, data and technology team of circa 200 staff and involves the exploitation of new, disruptive technologies including Machine Learning and AI, together with more established tech, to deliver a user centred service which meets the IPO’s customers’ current and future expectations and aspirations for the services we provide.

role_appointments.title: Sian-Nia Davies - Chief Digital Data & Technology Officer (CDDTO)

details.body: This role is responsible for all areas of domestic IP rights policy and legislation including Patents, Trade Marks, Designs and Copyright to support UK innovation through IP.

role_appointments.title: Chris Mills - Copyright and IP Enforcement Director, IPO

details.body: This role is responsible for Governance, Planning and Strategy (including secretariat, audit and risk functions); and Project and Programme Management. Encouraging, empowering and enabling innovation and growth through IP by supporting business, researching and understanding IP users, creating a robust evidence base, encouraging innovation and the use of IP and helping the IPO to get its message across successfully.

details.body:
The role of our Steering Board is to advise ministers, through our director general, on our strategies and performance (including targets) as set out in our Corporate Plan. It also provides guidance from a commercial standpoint on our operation and development across a range of issues.

During the last 12 months, the Steering Board has provided advice and guidance on a wide range of topics, such as our Corporate Plan, Agency Targets, Intellectual Property Policy, Accounts and Risk Management.

The Steering Board meets 6 times a year.

details.body:
Charity Commission Board members are ultimately responsible for all that the commission does. They set the commission’s values, business direction and strategy and make sure it acts fairly, responsibly, transparently, proportionately and ethically. Board members see that the commission maintains its integrity and independence. They identify and manage risks and listen and respond to stakeholders.

The commission’s Board members also:

  • monitor how the commission meets its statutory objectives and uses its legal powers
  • consider the commission’s management team’s performance, governance standards and delivery against plans
  • focus on maximising the commission’s impact and effectiveness
  • make sure the commission uses public funds prudently

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

details.body: The chief operating officer is responsible for the operation of corporate services within the IPO.
This role is responsible for all areas of domestic IP rights policy and legislation including Patents, Trade Marks, Designs and Copyright to support UK innovation through IP.

This role is responsible for a number of different corporate functions including: HR and Organisational Development (including operational and strategic HR, lean and continuous improvement, internal communications); Estates and Facilities Management (including office services, sustainability, and design and print solutions); Governance, Planning and Strategy (including secretariat, audit and risk functions); and Project and Programme Management.

Director of International Policy

This role is responsible for the Office’s international activities. This includes managing the IPO’s relationships with multilateral institutions such as WIPO, and the Office’s bilateral relationships and cooperation with key global markets such as China, North America, ASEAN, Brazil and India. It also provides support and awareness raising for UK businesses who are exporting or are looking to export - the UK’s IP attaché network is a key part of this work. The IPO’s preparation for the UK’s exit from the EU and our new international trade relationships are also major parts of this role.
This role develops and carries out different business strategies for the IPO.

The chief executive is the leader of the IPO and is responsible for the day-to-day management of the organisation.

The Comptroller General is formally responsible for the operation of the IPO and supervising the quality of its accounting and financial reporting.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • intellectual property (including copyright, trademarks, patents, design, enforcement)
  • Co-chair of PBS (Professional and Business Services) council
  • cross-cutting EU engagement with Lord Livingston – each BIS Minister will lead on EU engagement on areas within their policy brief
  • all BIS business in the Lords – excluding trade/exports and science/higher education

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills and Minister for Intellectual Property

The Deputy Chief Executive is responsible for the UK national patent, trade mark and registered design systems.

ordered_roles.title: Deputy Chief Executive and Director of Services, IPO

details.body:
BIS

Responsibilities for BIS include:

  • intellectual property
  • EU/single market
    • single market in goods and services
    • digital single market (Lords lead)
    • Competitiveness Council
  • corporate governance, company law and Companies House
  • postal affairs and Post Offices
  • BIS better regulation
  • BIS corporate (including UKSBS)
  • BIS Lords business

DCMS

  • data protection, including sponsorship of the Information Commissioner’s Office
  • The National Archives (in the process of transferring from the Lord Chancellor)
  • nuisance calls

DCMS Lords business – excluding internet safety and security

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for BIS and DCMS and Minister for Intellectual Property

details.body:
The Digital Data & Technology Directorate is central to the IPO’s journey of transformation and is charged with the creation of a world leading technology platform and service that can provide businesses and citizens with the tools they need to harness the power of IP.

The CDDTO ensures that the IPO’s strategic ambitions are fully supported by its tech capability and operating model.

This includes leading a committed, multi-disciplinary digital, data and technology team of circa 200 staff and involves the exploitation of new, disruptive technologies including Machine Learning and AI, together with more established tech, to deliver a user centred service which meets the IPO’s customers’ current and future expectations and aspirations for the services we provide.

The role of our Steering Board is to advise ministers, through our director general, on our strategies and performance (including targets) as set out in our Corporate Plan. It also provides guidance from a commercial standpoint on our operation and development across a range of issues.

During the last 12 months, the Steering Board has provided advice and guidance on a wide range of topics, such as our Corporate Plan, Agency Targets, Intellectual Property Policy, Accounts and Risk Management.

The Steering Board meets 6 times a year.

The Acting Chief Executive is the leader of the IPO and is responsible for the day-to-day management of the organisation.

The Comptroller General is formally responsible for the operation of the IPO and supervising the quality of its accounting and financial reporting.

ordered_roles.title: Acting Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • energy (with the Minister for Industry and Energy)
    • nuclear
    • oil and gas, including shale gas
    • low carbon generation
    • security of supply
    • electricity and gas wholesale markets and networks
    • energy efficiency and heat, including fuel poverty
    • smart meters and smart systems
    • international energy
    • energy security, including resilience and emergency planning
  • intellectual property
  • EU single market
  • Lords lead on all BEIS issues

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Energy and Intellectual Property

The Deputy CEO and Director of Operational Delivery is responsible for all of our registered rights functions, patents, trade marks and designs, including the associated tribunal and mediation services. This role also has responsibility for policy concerning the registered rights and unregistered designs.
